intro: Created in 1971 under Local Law 12, the Taxi and Limousine Commission is the Charter-mandated agency responsible for licensing and regulating New York City's medallion taxis, for-hire vehicles, commuter vans, and paratransit vehicles. Its nine-member Board, led by a salaried Chair, oversees a licensee base of more than 200,000 drivers and vehicle owners who together complete roughly one million trips a day across the five boroughs.

cardHighlights.function: Licenses and regulates taxis and for-hire vehicles
cardHighlights.scale: Oversees drivers completing roughly one million trips a day
